Fig. 6. Success rate vs. execution time across all methods. Points closer to the top-left indicate better overall performance. VAP-TAMP achieves the highest success rate with the lowest execution time.
Fig. 7. Success rates by task. VAP-TAMP maintains consistent performance across all tasks, while baselines show larger variance.
Fig. 9. Failure mode distribution across all methods.
Fig. 10. Situation handling evaluation in simulation comparing verification strategies.
TABLE II SITUATION INJECTION PROBABILITIES FOR SIMULATION EXPERIMENTS. EACH PROBABILITY DEFINES THE LIKELIHOOD OF THE CORRESPONDING SITUATION OCCURRING WHEN THE ACTION IS EXECUTED.

Fig. 2. VAP-TAMP System Overview. Given RGB-D observations and a goal description in natural language, VAP-TAMP builds a 3D point cloud (map), extracts an instance memory and scene graph (Section IV-A), and translates to PDDL for planning. During execution, preconditions and effects are verified before and after each action (Section IV-C), with uncertainty evaluation triggering active perception when needed (Section IV-B). Verification failures at either stage constitute situations that prompt state correction and replanning.

Fig. 4. VAP-TAMP integrates robot perception with domain knowledge by formulating the verifications of action preconditions and effects as visual question answering (VQA) queries. The left shows an example of precondition verification, where VAP-TAMP verifies the action preconditions one at a time. The precondition predicates are dynamically extracted from the PDDL-based description of the current action.
TABLE I ACTIONS, PRECONDITIONS, AND SITUATIONS (UNEXPECTED OUTCOMES) THAT VAP-TAMP MUST DETECT AND RECOVER FROM.
Fig. 5. Distribution of situations across tasks. Flows connect tasks (left) to situation (right), with occurrence count.
